name: linkedin_company_posts_scraper
title: LinkedIn Company Posts Scraper
description: Scrape recent public LinkedIn company posts from a company slug or URL, including post URL, text, author name, and published timestamp when exposed in public structured data.

seo.title: LinkedIn Company Posts Scraper Tool for Public Updates
seo.description: Scrape recent public LinkedIn company posts with Better Fetch, including post URL, text, author name, published timestamp, and source company URL.
seo.intro: LinkedIn Company Posts Scraper gives agents a focused public-company monitoring primitive. Provide a LinkedIn company slug or URL and the tool reads the logged-out company page, extracts public post objects from LinkedIn's structured data, and returns a clean list of recent updates ready for research, content analysis, or alerting workflows.

useCases.title: Company monitoring
useCases.description: Track recent public LinkedIn updates from competitors, prospects, customers, partners, or portfolio companies without using LinkedIn cookies.

useCases.title: Content analysis
useCases.description: Collect post text and URLs for brand messaging review, campaign research, product-launch monitoring, or executive briefing workflows.

useCases.title: Sales context
useCases.description: Give account agents recent company activity before outreach, renewal, or customer-success conversations.

faqs.question: Does LinkedIn Company Posts Scraper require login cookies?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 reads public structured data from the logged-out company page and does not use passwords, exported sessions, or private LinkedIn API tokens.

faqs.question: Does it paginate through every company post?
faqs.answer: No. Version 0.1 returns the recent posts LinkedIn exposes on the public company page. Deep pagination and full activity feeds remain future validated slices.

faqs.question: Does it return reactions or comments?
faqs.answer: No. This version returns post URL, text, author, and timestamp. Reaction counts, comment counts, and media attachment expansion should be added separately.

company_url.description: Public LinkedIn company URL. Use company_url or slug.

slug.description: LinkedIn company slug, such as openai. Use company_url or slug.

max_posts.description: Maximum recent public posts to return.

max_text_chars.description: Maximum characters per returned post text.

position.description: Post position in public structured data
